reelmower:blockhelp-mower-seatblade: Seat a reel blade or snow auger
reelmower:grindwheel-not-powered: The grinding wheel isn't spinning — connect it to a mechanical power source.
reelmower:hotkey-cutmode: Reel mower: cycle cut mode (while pushing)
reelmower:blade-worn: Blade worn out — repair by picking up and re-forging
reelmower:guide-assembly-title: Reel Mower & Snowblower: Some Assembly Required
reelmower:guide-assembly-text:
Nothing here comes pre-built.
Every piece of this mower gets made and put together by hand — including which wood it's made of. Here's the whole chain, start to finish.

1. Smith the blade
Forge a blade blank at the anvil from two ingots of the same metal, then finish it off in the crafting grid with a hammer and chisel.
Need — Anvil: 2× ingot (same metal). Grid: 1× blade blank + hammer + chisel.
Building a snowblower instead? Forge the very same blade blank, but in the grid move the chisel to the bottom-right corner (the reel blade puts it bottom-left) — hammer + blank on top, chisel bottom-right — and you get a snow auger head instead of a reel blade.
Need — Anvil: 2× ingot (same metal). Grid: 1× blade blank + hammer + chisel (chisel bottom-right).

2. Build the frame
Craft the frame in the grid from two metal plates, an ingot, and two rusty gears. Iron only — it has to be rigid enough to carry the blade.
Need — Grid: 2× iron plate + 1× iron ingot + 2× rusty gear.

3. Carve the shaft
Grab any log — all twelve wood types work — and turn it into a shaft in the crafting grid with a chisel and a hammer. The shaft also takes six iron bits (the yoke and the metal banding on the handle) and two plain cloth (the grip wraps), so have those on hand before you craft. Whatever wood you pick is what you'll see on the finished mower, so choose for looks if you want to.
Need — Grid: 1× log (any wood) + 6× iron bit + 2× plain cloth + chisel + hammer.

Optional — color the grips: craft the shaft together with a piece of dyed cloth in the crafting grid — any of the 13 cloth colors, set side by side. Dye plain cloth with a dye in a barrel first (the vanilla way), then bring that colored cloth here. The grip color carries all the way through assembly to the finished mower; plain cloth resets it back to default linen, and you can re-dye any time.
Need — Barrel: dye + plain cloth (the vanilla way). Grid: 1× shaft + 1× colored cloth.

4. Hammer it together
Set the frame down on the ground. Hold a hammer in your main hand and equip the shaft in your offhand, then tap leftmouse (attack) on the frame three separate times — tap it, don't hold it down, so each swing plays out fully — no sneak needed, just walk up and swing, same as hitting an anvil. That's three nails total, one consumed per hit, so make sure you're carrying at least that many before you start. Third hit, and the frame becomes a bladeless mower right there on the spot.
Need — On the ground: placed frame + shaft (offhand) + hammer (main hand) + 3× nails & strips (any metal, one spent per hit).

5. Seat the head
Hold the reel blade — or a snow auger — in your main hand and leftmouse (attack) the bladeless mower — the same swing you used to hammer the frame. Seat a reel blade and it's a mower; seat a snow auger and it's a snowblower. That's it — your machine, your wood, your metal.
Need — On the ground: placed bladeless mower + 1× finished reel blade OR snow auger (held).

Optional — fit a bed knife: for a cleaner shear, smith a bed knife from a single ingot of any metal (its own tier, independent of the reel) and install it — set the finished mower on the ground and leftmouse (attack) it while holding the bed knife. A copper bed knife sits happily on a steel-reel mower; the edge keeps its own metal. To pull it off, shift + rightmouse click the placed mower with an empty hand — the bed knife always comes off before the reel blade does.
Need — Anvil: 1× ingot (any metal). On the ground: placed mower + the bed knife (held).

Snowblower only — fit a chute: a snow auger needs a chute to do anything — it's the auger's counterpart to the bed knife, except it mounts on top and is required, not optional. Craft the chute in the grid: 3 oak boards across the top, 2 copper bits + a straight chute + 2 copper bits across the middle, 3 iron plates across the bottom. Then install it — set the snow-auger machine on the ground and leftmouse (attack) it while holding the chute. With the chute on, push the auger through snow to clear a path and pile that snow off to one side; tap reelmowercutmode while pushing to flip which side it throws to. Pull it off with an empty hand via shift + rightmouse — the chute always comes off before the auger. A chute only fits a snow auger, and a bed knife only fits a reel blade — never mixed.
Need — Grid: 3× oak board + 2× copper bits (each side slot) + 1× straight chute + 3× iron plate. On the ground: placed snow-auger machine + the chute (held).

Putting it to work
The finished machine doesn't cut in your hands anymore — it works as a push cart. rightmouse click the ground to set it down as a rideable cart. Grab it and walk: the wheels and the reel (or auger) spin as it rolls. A mower cuts whatever's in front of it going forward or back; a snowblower clears the snow ahead and hurls it out the chute to the side. Set the mode on the fly with reelmowercutmode while pushing — for a mower that cycles Trim → Remove → Clear, for a snowblower it flips the chute left/right. Either way the head wears down the same way swinging it by hand used to. When you're done, sneak + empty-hand rightmouse click the cart to pack it back up — every part, dye, and its remaining sharpness comes back intact.
Need — Open ground: 1× finished mower (nothing is consumed — it packs back up).

Want a different wood after the fact?
If there's a blade in it, pop that off first with an empty hand (shift + rightmouse click) — and if a bed knife is fitted, that first empty-hand click takes the bed knife, so click once more for the blade. Then hold a chisel in your main hand, a hammer in your offhand, and rightmouse click the bare bladeless mower (sneak not required here either) — the shaft comes back to you in one piece, same wood it always was, ready to swap for something else. The nails don't come back perfectly though — pulled-out nails are a bit bent up, so each one has a chance of not being reusable. Whatever metal you used going in is what comes back out, copper stays copper, steel stays steel.
Need — On the ground: placed bladeless mower + chisel (main hand) + hammer (offhand). Returns the shaft; some pulled nails may be too bent to reuse.
reelmower:handbook-mowerframe-text: This is the skeleton, not the finished tool. Set it down on the ground, then hold a hammer in your main hand with a shaft (made from any log) in your offhand and hammer it on — three hits, three nails total. See the Reel Mower & Snowblower assembly guide for the full walkthrough.
reelmower:handbook-mowershaft-text: The handle, carved from whatever log you had on hand — worked in the grid with a chisel and hammer, plus six iron bits (the yoke and handle banding) and two plain cloth (the grip wraps). Hammer it onto a placed frame to start building a mower — the wood you used here is the wood you'll see on the finished thing. Want colored grips? Craft the shaft together with a piece of cloth (any of the 13 dye colors) to dye the cloth wraps; the color carries all the way through to the finished mower, and plain cloth resets them. See the Reel Mower & Snowblower assembly guide for the full walkthrough.
reelmower:handbook-reelmower-bladeless-t: Frame and shaft, joined — just needs a head. Hold a finished reel blade (for a mower) or a snow auger (for a snowblower) and <hk>leftmouse</hk> (attack) this (while it's on the ground) to seat it. Changed your mind on the wood? A chisel and a hammer will pull the shaft back off. See the Reel Mower & Snowblower assembly guide for the full walkthrough.
reelmower:handbook-bedknife-text: An optional lower shearing bar that the spinning reel cuts against — like a real reel mower. Smith one from a single ingot of any metal (its own tier, independent of the reel), then install it: set a finished mower on the ground and <hk>leftmouse</hk> (attack) it while holding the bed knife. A copper bed knife goes on a steel mower just fine; the edge keeps its own metal. To pull it back off, <hk>shift</hk> + <hk>rightmouse</hk> click the placed mower with an empty hand — same as taking the reel blade off, except the bed knife sits in front and always comes off first. It drops back out in the metal you made it from. Note: a bed knife only fits a reel-blade mower, not a snow auger.
reelmower:handbook-snowauger-text: The snowblower's answer to the reel blade — a helical auger that scoops snow instead of shearing grass. Forge it just like a reel blade (a blade blank at the anvil), but in the crafting grid set the chisel in the bottom-right corner instead of the left. Seat it into a bladeless mower the same way — hold it and leftmouse (attack) the placed bladeless mower — and the machine becomes a snowblower. On its own the auger does nothing: it needs a chute mounted on top before it'll move any snow. See the Reel Mower & Snowblower assembly guide for the full walkthrough.
reelmower:handbook-chute-text: The snow auger's top attachment — the counterpart to the reel blade's bed knife. The auger won't move any snow until the chute is mounted: set a finished snow-auger mower on the ground and <hk>leftmouse</hk> (attack) it while holding the chute. With it on, pushing the auger through snow clears the drift ahead and piles that same snow off to one side; tap <hk>reelmowercutmode</hk> while pushing to switch which side it throws to (left or right). To pull it back off, <hk>shift</hk> + <hk>rightmouse</hk> click the placed mower with an empty hand — the chute sits on top and always comes off before the auger. A chute only fits a snow-auger mower, never a reel blade.
reelmower:handbook-reelmower-text: Every piece of this — frame, shaft, blade — was assembled by hand, and the wood you see is whatever log you picked for the shaft. It doesn't cut in your hands — it mows as a push cart. Right-click the ground to set it down as a rideable cart; grab it and mow as you walk, wheels and reel spinning as it rolls. Pick the cut (Trim, Remove, or Clear) on the fly with reelmowercutmode while pushing — no need to choose before you set it down. Sneak + empty-hand right-click packs it back up with every part and its remaining sharpness intact. Hang it on a Dowels peg (or a regular Tool Rack) when you're done for the day. Built with a snow auger and a chute instead of a reel blade? Then it's a snowblower — same push-cart idea, but it clears the snow ahead and hurls it out the side, and the cut-mode key aims the chute left or right instead of changing the cut. See the Reel Mower & Snowblower assembly guide for the full walkthrough.
